c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-gFHBZW-family-display c-fDhfVa-fwWvhp-size-6 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2 c-fDhfVa-icpNGWS-css">The physics behind spring animations</h1><time itemprop="datepublished" datetime="2020-06-23T08:00:00.000Z"><span class="c-PJLV c-lbKYOI">Jun 23, 2020</span><div aria-hidden="true" class="c-PJLV c-PJLV-icCitdK-css"><span class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-kGqoHr-family-mono c-fDhfVa-icJbem-size-1 c-fDhfVa-duxZVi-variant-tertiary c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2 c-fDhfVa-ikWdxHE-css">Jun 23, 2020</span></div></time></div><div class="c-PJLV c-cmpvrW c-PJLV-ijeeuID-css"><div class="c-dhzjXW c-dhzjXW-gjemnU-alignItems-start c-dhzjXW-iTKOFX-direction-column c-dhzjXW-gziqpR-gap-5 c-dhzjXW-kVNAnR-wrap-nowrap c-kedQaz"><p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">In the past few months, I've become a big fan of Framer Motion. After looking at how I could use it <a href="/posts/framer-motion-emotion" class="c-kySERo c-kySERo-iGeyqa-underline-true c-kySERo-igJWTOZ-css">to animate my styled-components</a>, I've been tinkering around spring animations and rebuilt almost all the transitions and animations of components in several UI projects. While showcasing the result to some fellow developers I got some questions around the meaning of some of the terms and options used to set up a spring animation like <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">mass</strong>, <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">stiffness</strong>, and <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">damping</strong>. Most of them were setting them without really knowing how they were influencing the resulting animation.
Lucky for them, I used to study maths and physics in college and was able to bring light on the physics behind this type of animation.</p>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">This article aims to explain how a spring animation in a library like Framer Motion works, the laws of physics that are behind it, and the relation between the different options you can set for a spring animation.</p>
<aside class="c-kKupis c-kKupis-kQOVSk-variant-info c-kKupis-ibPddok-css"><div data-testid="callout-icon-info" class="c-kirPJY c-kirPJY-fTjxTx-variant-info"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" role="img" class="c-PJLV c-PJLV-bHjGOQ-variant-default c-PJLV-hKgDZV-outline-true c-PJLV-gyinzs-size-4"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="10"></circle><path d="M12 16V11.5"></path><path d="M12 8.01172V8.00172"></path></svg></div><div class="c-dhzjXW c-dhzjXW-gjemnU-alignItems-start c-dhzjXW-iTKOFX-direction-column c-dhzjXW-kSZaEC-gap-6 c-dhzjXW-kVNAnR-wrap-nowrap"><p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">This is the <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">very first time</strong> I'm using something I've learned in physics class in college 😅, everything is possible!</p><p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">Also, I only studied this subject in french and never wrote about it in english, if there are any misused terms or definitions, please let me know and I'll update the post with the appropriate fixes.</p></div></aside>
<section id="hookes-law-section"><div class="c-dhzjXW c-dhzjXW-jroWjL-alignItems-center c-dhzjXW-ejCoEP-direction-row c-dhzjXW-bXfaDp-gap-1 c-dhzjXW-kVNAnR-wrap-nowrap c-dhzjXW-iZoSbU-css"><h2 id="hookes-law" class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-cdSgsb-size-4 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-gYdJvx-weight-4">Hooke's Law<a href="#hookes-law" class="c-kySERo c-kySERo-iGeyqa-underline-true c-kySERo-igJWTOZ-css anchor-link space-window-top"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" width="16" height="16" class="anchor-icon"><path d="M10 19.0004L9.82843 19.1719C8.26634 20.734 5.73368 20.734 4.17158 19.1719L3.82843 18.8288C2.26634 17.2667 2.26633 14.734 3.82843 13.1719L7.17158 9.8288C8.73368 8.2667 11.2663 8.2667 12.8284 9.8288L13.1716 10.1719C13.8252 10.8256 14.2053 11.6491 14.312 12.5004" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"></path><path d="M9.68799 12.5004C9.79463 13.3516 10.1748 14.1752 10.8284 14.8288L11.1715 15.1719C12.7336 16.734 15.2663 16.734 16.8284 15.1719L20.1715 11.8288C21.7336 10.2667 21.7336 7.73404 20.1715 6.17194L19.8284 5.8288C18.2663 4.2667 15.7336 4.2667 14.1715 5.8288L14 6.00037" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"></path></svg></a></h2><div class="c-PJLV c-PJLV-iECYnc-css"></div></div>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">First of all, a spring animation has this name because the animation itself follows the physics of a spring or what we also call a <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">Harmonic Oscillator</strong>. This term and the math surrounding it might seem very scary and complicated but bare with me, I'll break down everything as simply as possible.
When I was in college, we defined a Harmonic Oscillator as follows:</p>
<blockquote class="c-cZflSx"><div class="c-PJLV c-dErIMx">
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">a system that experiences a force (F) proportional to a displacement x when displaced from its equilibrium.</p>
</div></blockquote>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">The formula for such force is called Hooke's Law and it is defined as follows:</p>
<div class="c-PJLV c-kreyRl c-kreyRl-cNNFmd-depth-1 c-PJLV-ijxdteJ-css"><pre class="c-dGhWkF prism-code language-"><div data-testid="line" class="c-QIkcn token-line"><div data-testid="number-line" class="c-eotEYs">1</div><span class="c-hJePes"><span data-testid="content-line" class="token plain">F = -k*x</span></span></div></pre></div>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">where <code class="c-kEZxkv">k</code> is a positive constant called <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">stiffness</strong> which we can also write as:</p>
<aside class="c-kKupis c-kKupis-cPfNZW-variant-danger c-kKupis-ibPddok-css"><div data-testid="callout-icon-danger" class="c-kirPJY c-kirPJY-hTvZpY-variant-danger"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" role="img" class="c-PJLV c-PJLV-bHjGOQ-variant-default c-PJLV-hKgDZV-outline-true c-PJLV-gyinzs-size-4"><path d="M21.5 12.6863V11.3137C21.5 9.67871 21.5 8.8612 21.1955 8.12612C20.891 7.39104 20.313 6.81297 19.1569 5.65685L18.3431 4.84315C17.187 3.68702 16.609 3.10896 15.8739 2.80448C15.1388 2.5 14.3213 2.5 12.6863 2.5H11.3137C9.67871 2.5 8.8612 2.5 8.12612 2.80448C7.39104 3.10896 6.81297 3.68702 5.65685 4.84315L4.84315 5.65685C3.68702 6.81298 3.10896 7.39104 2.80448 8.12612C2.5 8.8612 2.5 9.67871 2.5 11.3137V12.6863C2.5 14.3213 2.5 15.1388 2.80448 15.8739C3.10896 16.609 3.68702 17.187 4.84315 18.3431L5.65685 19.1569C6.81297 20.313 7.39104 20.891 8.12612 21.1955C8.8612 21.5 9.67871 21.5 11.3137 21.5H12.6863C14.3213 21.5 15.1388 21.5 15.8739 21.1955C16.609 20.891 17.187 20.313 18.3431 19.1569L19.1569 18.3431C20.313 17.187 20.891 16.609 21.1955 15.8739C21.5 15.1388 21.5 14.3213 21.5 12.6863Z"></path><path d="M12 8L12 12.5"></path><path d="M12 15.9883V15.9983"></path></svg></div><div class="c-dhzjXW c-dhzjXW-gjemnU-alignItems-start c-dhzjXW-iTKOFX-direction-column c-dhzjXW-kSZaEC-gap-6 c-dhzjXW-kVNAnR-wrap-nowrap"><p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2"><strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">force = negative stiffness * displacement</strong></p></div></aside>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">What that means is that:</p>
<ul class="c-iUzKch c-iUzKch-jmtdjB-variant-unordered">
<li class="c-fvFIDZ"><div data-list-item="true" class="c-PJLV"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" role="img" class="c-PJLV c-PJLV-fBaGsk-variant-info c-PJLV-hKgDZV-outline-true c-PJLV-gyinzs-size-4"><path d="M18.5 12L4.99997 12"></path><path d="M13 18C13 18 19 13.5811 19 12C19 10.4188 13 6 13 6"></path></svg></div><div>if we pull the spring (i.e. x > 0 ) to a certain distance away from its equilibrium, it will start to move</div></li>
<li class="c-fvFIDZ"><div data-list-item="true" class="c-PJLV"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" role="img" class="c-PJLV c-PJLV-fBaGsk-variant-info c-PJLV-hKgDZV-outline-true c-PJLV-gyinzs-size-4"><path d="M18.5 12L4.99997 12"></path><path d="M13 18C13 18 19 13.5811 19 12C19 10.4188 13 6 13 6"></path></svg></div><div>if we don't pull it, it won't move (i.e. x = 0)</div></li>
</ul>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">However, maybe you might have heard at school or on one of the many science-focused Youtube channels on that force is the object's mass times its acceleration, which translates to the following formula:</p>
<div class="c-PJLV c-kreyRl c-kreyRl-cNNFmd-depth-1 c-PJLV-ijxdteJ-css"><pre class="c-dGhWkF prism-code language-"><div data-testid="line" class="c-QIkcn token-line"><div data-testid="number-line" class="c-eotEYs">1</div><span class="c-hJePes"><span data-testid="content-line" class="token plain">F = m*a</span></span></div></pre></div>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">where <code class="c-kEZxkv">m</code> is the <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">mass</strong> and <code class="c-kEZxkv">a</code> is the <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">acceleration</strong>.</p>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">Thus given this formula and the formula above, we can deduct that:</p>
<div class="c-PJLV c-kreyRl c-kreyRl-cNNFmd-depth-1 c-PJLV-ijxdteJ-css"><pre class="c-dGhWkF prism-code language-"><div data-testid="line" class="c-QIkcn token-line"><div data-testid="number-line" class="c-eotEYs">1</div><span class="c-hJePes"><span data-testid="content-line" class="token plain">m*a = -k*x</span></span></div></pre></div>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">which is equivalent to</p>
<div class="c-PJLV c-kreyRl c-kreyRl-cNNFmd-depth-1 c-PJLV-ijxdteJ-css"><pre class="c-dGhWkF prism-code language-"><div data-testid="line" class="c-QIkcn token-line"><div data-testid="number-line" class="c-eotEYs">1</div><span class="c-hJePes"><span data-testid="content-line" class="token plain">a = -k *x / m</span></span></div></pre></div>
<aside class="c-kKupis c-kKupis-cPfNZW-variant-danger c-kKupis-ibPddok-css"><div data-testid="callout-icon-danger" class="c-kirPJY c-kirPJY-hTvZpY-variant-danger"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" role="img" class="c-PJLV c-PJLV-bHjGOQ-variant-default c-PJLV-hKgDZV-outline-true c-PJLV-gyinzs-size-4"><path d="M21.5 12.6863V11.3137C21.5 9.67871 21.5 8.8612 21.1955 8.12612C20.891 7.39104 20.313 6.81297 19.1569 5.65685L18.3431 4.84315C17.187 3.68702 16.609 3.10896 15.8739 2.80448C15.1388 2.5 14.3213 2.5 12.6863 2.5H11.3137C9.67871 2.5 8.8612 2.5 8.12612 2.80448C7.39104 3.10896 6.81297 3.68702 5.65685 4.84315L4.84315 5.65685C3.68702 6.81298 3.10896 7.39104 2.80448 8.12612C2.5 8.8612 2.5 9.67871 2.5 11.3137V12.6863C2.5 14.3213 2.5 15.1388 2.80448 15.8739C3.10896 16.609 3.68702 17.187 4.84315 18.3431L5.65685 19.1569C6.81297 20.313 7.39104 20.891 8.12612 21.1955C8.8612 21.5 9.67871 21.5 11.3137 21.5H12.6863C14.3213 21.5 15.1388 21.5 15.8739 21.1955C16.609 20.891 17.187 20.313 18.3431 19.1569L19.1569 18.3431C20.313 17.187 20.891 16.609 21.1955 15.8739C21.5 15.1388 21.5 14.3213 21.5 12.6863Z"></path><path d="M12 8L12 12.5"></path><path d="M12 15.9883V15.9983"></path></svg></div><div class="c-dhzjXW c-dhzjXW-gjemnU-alignItems-start c-dhzjXW-iTKOFX-direction-column c-dhzjXW-kSZaEC-gap-6 c-dhzjXW-kVNAnR-wrap-nowrap"><p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2"><strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">acceleration = negative stiffness * displacement / mass</strong></p></div></aside>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">We now have an equation from which we define the acceleration based on the displacement of our spring and the mass of the object attached to that spring. From the acceleration we can deduct the following:</p>
<ul class="c-iUzKch c-iUzKch-jmtdjB-variant-unordered">
<li class="c-fvFIDZ"><div data-list-item="true" class="c-PJLV"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" role="img" class="c-PJLV c-PJLV-fBaGsk-variant-info c-PJLV-hKgDZV-outline-true c-PJLV-gyinzs-size-4"><path d="M18.5 12L4.99997 12"></path><path d="M13 18C13 18 19 13.5811 19 12C19 10.4188 13 6 13 6"></path></svg></div><div>the velocity of the object at any given time</div></li>
<li class="c-fvFIDZ"><div data-list-item="true" class="c-PJLV"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" role="img" class="c-PJLV c-PJLV-fBaGsk-variant-info c-PJLV-hKgDZV-outline-true c-PJLV-gyinzs-size-4"><path d="M18.5 12L4.99997 12"></path><path d="M13 18C13 18 19 13.5811 19 12C19 10.4188 13 6 13 6"></path></svg></div><div>the position of the object at any given time</div></li>
</ul>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">To get the velocity of the object, you need to add the acceleration rate to the previously recorded velocity, which can translate to the following equation:</p>
<div class="c-PJLV c-kreyRl c-kreyRl-cNNFmd-depth-1 c-PJLV-ijxdteJ-css"><pre class="c-dGhWkF prism-code language-"><div data-testid="line" class="c-QIkcn token-line"><div data-testid="number-line" class="c-eotEYs">1</div><span class="c-hJePes"><span data-testid="content-line" class="token plain">v2 = v1 + a*t</span></span></div></pre></div>
<aside class="c-kKupis c-kKupis-cPfNZW-variant-danger c-kKupis-ibPddok-css"><div data-testid="callout-icon-danger" class="c-kirPJY c-kirPJY-hTvZpY-variant-danger"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" role="img" class="c-PJLV c-PJLV-bHjGOQ-variant-default c-PJLV-hKgDZV-outline-true c-PJLV-gyinzs-size-4"><path d="M21.5 12.6863V11.3137C21.5 9.67871 21.5 8.8612 21.1955 8.12612C20.891 7.39104 20.313 6.81297 19.1569 5.65685L18.3431 4.84315C17.187 3.68702 16.609 3.10896 15.8739 2.80448C15.1388 2.5 14.3213 2.5 12.6863 2.5H11.3137C9.67871 2.5 8.8612 2.5 8.12612 2.80448C7.39104 3.10896 6.81297 3.68702 5.65685 4.84315L4.84315 5.65685C3.68702 6.81298 3.10896 7.39104 2.80448 8.12612C2.5 8.8612 2.5 9.67871 2.5 11.3137V12.6863C2.5 14.3213 2.5 15.1388 2.80448 15.8739C3.10896 16.609 3.68702 17.187 4.84315 18.3431L5.65685 19.1569C6.81297 20.313 7.39104 20.891 8.12612 21.1955C8.8612 21.5 9.67871 21.5 11.3137 21.5H12.6863C14.3213 21.5 15.1388 21.5 15.8739 21.1955C16.609 20.891 17.187 20.313 18.3431 19.1569L19.1569 18.3431C20.313 17.187 20.891 16.609 21.1955 15.8739C21.5 15.1388 21.5 14.3213 21.5 12.6863Z"></path><path d="M12 8L12 12.5"></path><path d="M12 15.9883V15.9983"></path></svg></div><div class="c-dhzjXW c-dhzjXW-gjemnU-alignItems-start c-dhzjXW-iTKOFX-direction-column c-dhzjXW-kSZaEC-gap-6 c-dhzjXW-kVNAnR-wrap-nowrap"><p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2"><strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">velocity = old velocity + acceleration * time interval</strong></p></div></aside>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">Finally, we can get the position as it follows a similar principle: the position of the object is equal to the previously recorded position to which we add the velocity:</p>
<div class="c-PJLV c-kreyRl c-kreyRl-cNNFmd-depth-1 c-PJLV-ijxdteJ-css"><pre class="c-dGhWkF prism-code language-"><div data-testid="line" class="c-QIkcn token-line"><div data-testid="number-line" class="c-eotEYs">1</div><span class="c-hJePes"><span data-testid="content-line" class="token plain">p2 =  p1 + v*t</span></span></div></pre></div>
<aside class="c-kKupis c-kKupis-cPfNZW-variant-danger c-kKupis-ibPddok-css"><div data-testid="callout-icon-danger" class="c-kirPJY c-kirPJY-hTvZpY-variant-danger"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" role="img" class="c-PJLV c-PJLV-bHjGOQ-variant-default c-PJLV-hKgDZV-outline-true c-PJLV-gyinzs-size-4"><path d="M21.5 12.6863V11.3137C21.5 9.67871 21.5 8.8612 21.1955 8.12612C20.891 7.39104 20.313 6.81297 19.1569 5.65685L18.3431 4.84315C17.187 3.68702 16.609 3.10896 15.8739 2.80448C15.1388 2.5 14.3213 2.5 12.6863 2.5H11.3137C9.67871 2.5 8.8612 2.5 8.12612 2.80448C7.39104 3.10896 6.81297 3.68702 5.65685 4.84315L4.84315 5.65685C3.68702 6.81298 3.10896 7.39104 2.80448 8.12612C2.5 8.8612 2.5 9.67871 2.5 11.3137V12.6863C2.5 14.3213 2.5 15.1388 2.80448 15.8739C3.10896 16.609 3.68702 17.187 4.84315 18.3431L5.65685 19.1569C6.81297 20.313 7.39104 20.891 8.12612 21.1955C8.8612 21.5 9.67871 21.5 11.3137 21.5H12.6863C14.3213 21.5 15.1388 21.5 15.8739 21.1955C16.609 20.891 17.187 20.313 18.3431 19.1569L19.1569 18.3431C20.313 17.187 20.891 16.609 21.1955 15.8739C21.5 15.1388 21.5 14.3213 21.5 12.6863Z"></path><path d="M12 8L12 12.5"></path><path d="M12 15.9883V15.9983"></path></svg></div><div class="c-dhzjXW c-dhzjXW-gjemnU-alignItems-start c-dhzjXW-iTKOFX-direction-column c-dhzjXW-kSZaEC-gap-6 c-dhzjXW-kVNAnR-wrap-nowrap"><p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2"><strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">position = old position + velocity * time interval</strong></p></div></aside>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">For the time interval, as frontend developers, we might know it better as a <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">frame rate</strong> or <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">"frames per second"</strong>. Considering the smoothness of Framer Motion's animations we can assume that its spring animations runs at 60 frames per second thus a time interval that is constant and equal to <code class="c-kEZxkv">1/60</code> or <code class="c-kEZxkv">0.01666</code>.</p>
</section><section id="translating-the-maths-to-javascript-section"><div class="c-dhzjXW c-dhzjXW-jroWjL-alignItems-center c-dhzjXW-ejCoEP-direction-row c-dhzjXW-bXfaDp-gap-1 c-dhzjXW-kVNAnR-wrap-nowrap c-dhzjXW-iZoSbU-css"><h2 id="translating-the-maths-to-javascript" class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-cdSgsb-size-4 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-gYdJvx-weight-4">Translating the maths to Javascript<a href="#translating-the-maths-to-javascript" class="c-kySERo c-kySERo-iGeyqa-underline-true c-kySERo-igJWTOZ-css anchor-link space-window-top"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" width="16" height="16" class="anchor-icon"><path d="M10 19.0004L9.82843 19.1719C8.26634 20.734 5.73368 20.734 4.17158 19.1719L3.82843 18.8288C2.26634 17.2667 2.26633 14.734 3.82843 13.1719L7.17158 9.8288C8.73368 8.2667 11.2663 8.2667 12.8284 9.8288L13.1716 10.1719C13.8252 10.8256 14.2053 11.6491 14.312 12.5004" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"></path><path d="M9.68799 12.5004C9.79463 13.3516 10.1748 14.1752 10.8284 14.8288L11.1715 15.1719C12.7336 16.734 15.2663 16.734 16.8284 15.1719L20.1715 11.8288C21.7336 10.2667 21.7336 7.73404 20.1715 6.17194L19.8284 5.8288C18.2663 4.2667 15.7336 4.2667 14.1715 5.8288L14 6.00037" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"></path></svg></a></h2><div class="c-PJLV c-PJLV-iECYnc-css"></div></div>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">Now that we've done the math, you can see that by knowing the <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">mass</strong> of the object, the <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">stiffness</strong> and the <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">displacement</strong> of our spring, we can know the position of the object attached to that spring at any given time, i.e at any given frame.

97,92.06578790714156C305.77777777777777,91.51537145782851,305.9555555555555,90.97370752780006,306.1333333333333,90.44225697648761C306.31111111111113,89.91080642517518,306.4888888888889,89.38956925257877,306.6666666666667,88.8799422728398C306.84444444444443,88.37031529310082,307.0222222222222,87.87229850621925,307.2,87.38721724119442C307.3777777777778,86.90213597616957,307.5555555555556,86.42999023300145,307.73333333333335,85.972026850034C307.9111111111111,85.51406346706652,308.0888888888889,85.07028244429972,308.26666666666665,84.64184559499583C308.4444444444444,84.21340874569194,308.6222222222222,83.80031606985095,308.8,83.40363832626065C308.9777777777778,83.00696058267037,309.15555555555557,82.62669777133078,309.3333333333333,82.2638241031677C309.5111111111111,81.90095043500463,309.68888888888887,81.55546591001806,309.8666666666666,81.2282432617282C310.0444444444444,80.9010206134383,310.22222222222223,80.59205984184513,310.40000000000003,80.3021278619307C310.5777777777778,80.01219588201627,310.75555555555553,79.74129269378061,310.93333333333334,79.4900756621215C311.11111111111114,79.23885863046239,311.28888888888895,79.00732775537983,311.4666666666667,78.79602774741065C311.64444444444445,78.58472773944146,311.82222222222225,78.39365859858563,312,78.22324991812108C312.17777777777775,78.05284123765652,312.35555555555555,77.90309301758325,312.53333333333336,77.77431792287727C312.71111111111117,77.6455428281713,312.8888888888889,77.53774085883263,313.06666666666666,77.45110659914927C313.24444444444447,77.36447233946589,313.4222222222222,77.29900578943781,313.59999999999997,77.25478296204462C313.77777777777777,77.21056013465142,313.9555555555556,77.1875810298931,314.13333333333327,77.18580326000303C314.3111111111111,77.18402549011296,314.4888888888889,77.20344905509114,314.66666666666663,77.24391399391608C314.84444444444443,77.284378932741,315.0222222222222,77.3458852454127,315.2,77.42815687419336C315.3777777777778,77.51042850297404,315.5555555555556,77.61346544786369, </section><section id="taking-damping-into-account-section"><div class="c-dhzjXW c-dhzjXW-jroWjL-alignItems-center c-dhzjXW-ejCoEP-direction-row c-dhzjXW-bXfaDp-gap-1 c-dhzjXW-kVNAnR-wrap-nowrap c-dhzjXW-iZoSbU-css"><h2 id="taking-damping-into-account" class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-cdSgsb-size-4 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-gYdJvx-weight-4">Taking damping into account<a href="#taking-damping-into-account" class="c-kySERo c-kySERo-iGeyqa-underline-true c-kySERo-igJWTOZ-css anchor-link space-window-top"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" width="16" height="16" class="anchor-icon"><path d="M10 19.0004L9.82843 19.1719C8.26634 20.734 5.73368 20.734 4.17158 19.1719L3.82843 18.8288C2.26634 17.2667 2.26633 14.734 3.82843 13.1719L7.17158 9.8288C8.73368 8.2667 11.2663 8.2667 12.8284 9.8288L13.1716 10.1719C13.8252 10.8256 14.2053 11.6491 14.312 12.5004" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"></path><path d="M9.68799 12.5004C9.79463 13.3516 10.1748 14.1752 10.8284 14.8288L11.1715 15.1719C12.7336 16.734 15.2663 16.734 16.8284 15.1719L20.1715 11.8288C21.7336 10.2667 21.7336 7.73404 20.1715 6.17194L19.8284 5.8288C18.2663 4.2667 15.7336 4.2667 14.1715 5.8288L14 6.00037" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"></path></svg></a></h2><div class="c-PJLV c-PJLV-iECYnc-css"></div></div>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">While observing the visualization above, you might have wondered why is the spring animation never-ending as opposed to the ones you might have tried yourself with Framer Motion. That is because the math formulas we used to generate the position of the object were not taking into account friction and heat. If we want to obtain a spring animation that feels natural, we should see the movement of the object slowing down as time passes to eventually stop moving.
That is where the <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">damping</strong> comes into the picture. You might have seen this term when looking at the documentation of Framer Motion and wondered what it meant or does to the resulting spring animation, here's how we will define it:</p>
<blockquote class="c-cZflSx"><div class="c-PJLV c-dErIMx">
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">Damping is the force that slows down and eventually stops an oscillation by dissipating energy</p>
</div></blockquote>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">Its formula is:</p>
<div class="c-PJLV c-kreyRl c-kreyRl-cNNFmd-depth-1 c-PJLV-ijxdteJ-css"><pre class="c-dGhWkF prism-code language-"><div data-testid="line" class="c-QIkcn token-line"><div data-testid="number-line" class="c-eotEYs">1</div><span class="c-hJePes"><span data-testid="content-line" class="token plain">Fd = -d * v</span></span></div></pre></div>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">where <code class="c-kEZxkv">d</code> is the damping ratio and <code class="c-kEZxkv">v</code> the velocity</p>
<aside class="c-kKupis c-kKupis-cPfNZW-variant-danger c-kKupis-ibPddok-css"><div data-testid="callout-icon-danger" class="c-kirPJY c-kirPJY-hTvZpY-variant-danger"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" role="img" class="c-PJLV c-PJLV-bHjGOQ-variant-default c-PJLV-hKgDZV-outline-true c-PJLV-gyinzs-size-4"><path d="M21.5 12.6863V11.3137C21.5 9.67871 21.5 8.8612 21.1955 8.12612C20.891 7.39104 20.313 6.81297 19.1569 5.65685L18.3431 4.84315C17.187 3.68702 16.609 3.10896 15.8739 2.80448C15.1388 2.5 14.3213 2.5 12.6863 2.5H11.3137C9.67871 2.5 8.8612 2.5 8.12612 2.80448C7.39104 3.10896 6.81297 3.68702 5.65685 4.84315L4.84315 5.65685C3.68702 6.81298 3.10896 7.39104 2.80448 8.12612C2.5 8.8612 2.5 9.67871 2.5 11.3137V12.6863C2.5 14.3213 2.5 15.1388 2.80448 15.8739C3.10896 16.609 3.68702 17.187 4.84315 18.3431L5.65685 19.1569C6.81297 20.313 7.39104 20.891 8.12612 21.1955C8.8612 21.5 9.67871 21.5 11.3137 21.5H12.6863C14.3213 21.5 15.1388 21.5 15.8739 21.1955C16.609 20.891 17.187 20.313 18.3431 19.1569L19.1569 18.3431C20.313 17.187 20.891 16.609 21.1955 15.8739C21.5 15.1388 21.5 14.3213 21.5 12.6863Z"></path><path d="M12 8L12 12.5"></path><path d="M12 15.9883V15.9983"></path></svg></div><div class="c-dhzjXW c-dhzjXW-gjemnU-alignItems-start c-dhzjXW-iTKOFX-direction-column c-dhzjXW-kSZaEC-gap-6 c-dhzjXW-kVNAnR-wrap-nowrap"><p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2"><strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">damping Force = negative damping * velocity</strong></p></div></aside>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">Taking damping into account will bring some changes to the acceleration formula we established in the first part.
We know that</p>
<div class="c-PJLV c-kreyRl c-kreyRl-cNNFmd-depth-1 c-PJLV-ijxdteJ-css"><pre class="c-dGhWkF prism-code language-"><div data-testid="line" class="c-QIkcn token-line"><div data-testid="number-line" class="c-eotEYs">1</div><span class="c-hJePes"><span data-testid="content-line" class="token plain">F = m*a</span></span></div></pre></div>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">However, F here is equal to the spring force and the damping force, instead of just the spring force, thus:</p>
<div class="c-PJLV c-kreyRl c-kreyRl-cNNFmd-depth-1 c-PJLV-ijxdteJ-css"><pre class="c-dGhWkF prism-code language-"><div data-testid="line" class="c-QIkcn token-line"><div data-testid="number-line" class="c-eotEYs">1</div><span class="c-hJePes"><span data-testid="content-line" class="token plain">Fs + Fd = m*a -> a = (Fs + Fd)/m</span></span></div></pre></div>

100%);height:100px;width:100px;border-radius:10px"></div></div></div></div>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">As you can see we now have a spring animation that eventually stops due to damping <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">dissipating the energy out of the system</strong>. The chart above showcases this by <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">converging towards a final "resting position"</strong>. By increasing the damping slider to a high value you can observe that the object subject to spring animation tends to converge to the "resting position" way faster than for a lower damping value.</p>
</section><section id="a-real-life-example-section"><div class="c-dhzjXW c-dhzjXW-jroWjL-alignItems-center c-dhzjXW-ejCoEP-direction-row c-dhzjXW-bXfaDp-gap-1 c-dhzjXW-kVNAnR-wrap-nowrap c-dhzjXW-iZoSbU-css"><h2 id="a-real-life-example" class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-cdSgsb-size-4 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-gYdJvx-weight-4">A real-life example<a href="#a-real-life-example" class="c-kySERo c-kySERo-iGeyqa-underline-true c-kySERo-igJWTOZ-css anchor-link space-window-top"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" width="16" height="16" class="anchor-icon"><path d="M10 19.0004L9.82843 19.1719C8.26634 20.734 5.73368 20.734 4.17158 19.1719L3.82843 18.8288C2.26634 17.2667 2.26633 14.734 3.82843 13.1719L7.17158 9.8288C8.73368 8.2667 11.2663 8.2667 12.8284 9.8288L13.1716 10.1719C13.8252 10.8256 14.2053 11.6491 14.312 12.5004" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"></path><path d="M9.68799 12.5004C9.79463 13.3516 10.1748 14.1752 10.8284 14.8288L11.1715 15.1719C12.7336 16.734 15.2663 16.734 16.8284 15.1719L20.1715 11.8288C21.7336 10.2667 21.7336 7.73404 20.1715 6.17194L19.8284 5.8288C18.2663 4.2667 15.7336 4.2667 14.1715 5.8288L14 6.00037" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"></path></svg></a></h2><div class="c-PJLV c-PJLV-iECYnc-css"></div></div>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">By default, Framer Motion sets the stiffness of the spring animation to <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">100</strong>, the damping to <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">10</strong>, and the mass to <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">1</strong> <a href="https://www.framer.com/api/motion/types/#spring" class="c-kySERo c-kySERo-ceiqTR-external-true c-kySERo-iGeyqa-underline-true c-kySERo-igFbDfV-css">according to the docs</a>. Below, I wrote an animated <code class="c-kEZxkv">Button</code> component that is closer to a real-life example that you might want to implement in your UI projects. Now that you know what <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">mass</strong>, <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">stiffness</strong> and <strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">damping</strong> you can try to fine-tune your spring animation.</p>

<li class="c-fvFIDZ"><div data-list-item="true" class="c-PJLV"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" role="img" class="c-PJLV c-PJLV-fBaGsk-variant-info c-PJLV-hKgDZV-outline-true c-PJLV-gyinzs-size-4"><path d="M18.5 12L4.99997 12"></path><path d="M13 18C13 18 19 13.5811 19 12C19 10.4188 13 6 13 6"></path></svg></div><div><strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">velocity</strong>: I considered the initial velocity to be equal to 0 in our examples above</div></li>
<li class="c-fvFIDZ"><div data-list-item="true" class="c-PJLV"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" role="img" class="c-PJLV c-PJLV-fBaGsk-variant-info c-PJLV-hKgDZV-outline-true c-PJLV-gyinzs-size-4"><path d="M18.5 12L4.99997 12"></path><path d="M13 18C13 18 19 13.5811 19 12C19 10.4188 13 6 13 6"></path></svg></div><div><strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">restSpeed</strong></div></li>

</ul><p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">They are all defined <a href="https://www.framer.com/api/motion/types/#spring" class="c-kySERo c-kySERo-ceiqTR-external-true c-kySERo-iGeyqa-underline-true c-kySERo-igFbDfV-css">in the documentation</a>, and I invite you to add them to the playground above to see how they influence the resulting animation.</p></div></aside>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2"><strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">Want to learn more the math/physics behind animations you see on a day-to-day basis?</strong></p>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2">I dedicated an entire blog post about <a href="/posts/cubic-bezier-from-math-to-motion/" class="c-kySERo c-kySERo-iGeyqa-underline-true c-kySERo-igJWTOZ-css">the math behind Cubic Béziers</a> that contains slick visualizations that easily explain how the motion these complex formulas define is obtained!</p>
<p class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-hYBDYy-variant-default c-fDhfVa-kHnRXL-weight-2"><strong class="c-fDhfVa c-fDhfVa-NUBYH-spaced-true c-fDhfVa-jFCKZD-family-default c-fDhfVa-grGuE-size-3 c-fDhfVa-eRpaQu-variant-primary c-fDhfVa-jmCwYG-weight-3">Want to learn more about Framer Motion?</strong></p>
